text.title: LinkedIn Ads Campaign Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ads-linkedin-ads methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Run LinkedIn ads ONLY where ICP LTV ≥ $5k AND seniority filter is Director+. Anything else fails the auction math.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn auction floor + premium CPC mean low-LTV / junior-audience campaigns burn budget without payback.

rule.statement: Always seed matched-account audiences before broad targeting; cold interest+title targeting MUST be capped at 25% of budget.
rule.rationale: Matched lists deliver 3-5x higher MQL rate than interest+title cold targeting.

rule.statement: Format MUST match funnel stage: TOFU → single image / video / document; MOFU → lead-gen form; BOFU → conversation ad / message ad to warm contacts only.
rule.rationale: Mismatched formats produce 50%+ wasted spend (conversation ads to cold = spam complaints).

rule.statement: Report cost-per-MQL and MQL→SQL rate; do not report cost-per-click or cost-per-lead alone.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn cost-per-lead can look fine while every lead is a student / wrong-region.

rule.statement: Frequency cap ≤ 4 impressions per week per user per campaign. Above that creative fatigues fast on a feed people visit weekly.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn users visit the feed once or twice a week; over 4 impressions = banner blindness + brand fatigue.
